status: a cluster of cases with fever and parotid gland swelling were reported from shasthamkotta and poruvazhy panchayats, blocks sooranadu (137 cases) and mynagapally (176 cases), sub-district kunnathur, district kollam. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases gradually increased in october–november. the index case was a 6-year-old male from poruvazhy panchayat who self-isolated for 15 days. children aged 5–6 years were most affected, and cases also occurred in adults. the outbreak spread rapidly through school buses, where children from different panchayats were in close, poorly ventilated contact, facilitating transmission. inter-school attendance and household contacts further amplified spread, making containment challenging. cases were treated symptomatically at health facilities and isolated at home. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

status: under surveillance
comments: Cases were reported from Village Vengara (CT), Sub- District Tirurangadi, District Malappuram. A 38-years-old male travelled to Kerala on 13/10/2025 from Ajman, UAE. On the next day (14/10/2025), he developed gastric discomfort and consulted a private hospital for the same. After a few days, he noticed two small blisters in the lower abdomen, followed by painful blisters in the genital region. On 21/10/2025, he consulted a private dermatologist in Chemmad, who clinically suspected Mpox and referred him to Manjeri Medical College for further evaluation and isolation. His wife, a 27- years-old female was also admitted in the isolation ward of the hospital. Samples of the patient and his wife were sent to NIV Pune, tested positive for Mpox (Clade I) by RT- PCR on 27.10.2025. Subsequently, both the cases tested negative for Mpox on 7.11.2025 and 15.11.2025. The male patient was discharged on 7.11.2025 and his wife was discharged on 17.11.2025. Public health actions included contact tracing and screening, isolation of the index case and his spouse at Manjeri Medical College, active surveillance of the residential area, and monitoring of all asymptomatic contacts.

Active search for fever cases was done in the affected area. Cases were in the age group 11-70 years. Cases were treated at CHC Suithakala. 24 samples were collected tested negative for Dengue and Malaria (RDT). Further, 2 blood samples collected tested positive for S. typhi (TyphiDot). Anti-larval activities, IRS and fogging was done. The public was made aware about protection from mosquitoes, not rearing pigs, maintaining cleanliness, drinking water from India Mark 2 hand pumps, drinking boiled water, sleeping under a mosquito net and wearing full sleeves clothes.

status: under surveillance
comments: A case was reported from Village Karukutty, Sub-District Aluva, District Ernakulam.. District RRT investigated the outbreak. A 24 -years-old male was admitted to Dept of General Medicine unit-5 isolation ward AIMS Kochi on 4.10.25 with clinical symptoms provisionally diagnosed as Mpox. The patient had a history of international travel and returned back to Kochi on 30.09.25. He was thermodynamically stable and on supportive medications. Blood in EDTA, Naso and oro pharyngeal swabs, swabs from lesions were collected and sent to NIV, Alappuzha tested positive for Mpox by RT-PCR on 07.10.25. Second samples (TS, NPS Plasma, Urine and CSF, lesion swab) sent to NIV Pune for confirmation and all samples tested negative on 15.10.25. The patient was discharged from AIMS Kochi on 16.10.25. The flight contacts details were taken from the Airport health officer. None of the contacts developed any symptom. Mental support and health awareness was given on control, hygiene, and sanitation.

comments: Cases presented with vomiting, nausea and some cases presented with loose motions from Village Kadalimunda, Sub-District Debagarh, District Deogarh. District RRT investigated the outbreak. status: under surveillance
comments: 2 cases were reported from Rajkot, Gujarat. A 37-year-old male with a history of international travel to Malaysia and Dubai, developed blisters on the face and rashes over the peri-anal region on 05-09-2025 while in Malaysia. Symptoms worsened, and he sought medical care on 10-09-2025 in Malaysia and on 11-09-2025 in Dubai. The patient returned back to India on 13-09-2025 and was admitted to Sterling hospital Rajkot. NPS/TS, scrapping from base of lesion and lesion swab samples were collected on 18-09-2025 and sent to the National Institute of Virology (NIV), Pune, which tested positive on 19-09-2025 for Mpox virus RT-PCR (Clade I). The patient is currently stable on room air. No further cases were detected during 21 days of follow-up. A total of three samples of contacts were collected and all tested negative for Mpox virus at NIV Pune. Public health actions included contact tracing and screening, isolation of the index case at Sterling Hospital, active surveillance of the residential area, and monitoring of all asymptomatic contacts.

comments: A 53-year-old female case reported with the symptoms of sudden onset of fever with abdominal discomfort from Village Katola Pathar (Katbal Pathar), Sub-District Sarupeta (Pt), District Bajali on 26.08.2025. District RRT investigated the outbreak. She was later admitted to FAAMCH on 02.09.2025 with worsening symptoms. Laboratory testing (IgM ELISA) on 09.09.2025 confirmed Leptospirosis; other tests for Hepatitis A & E, Scrub Typhus, Malaria, and HBsAg were negative. The patient succumbed on 09.09.2025 due to septic shock secondary to Leptospirosis. The likely source of infection was contaminated surroundings due to close animal-human contact. Public health actions included community awareness on zoonotic diseases, active case search, surveillance by AAM team, and veterinary sample collection for testing.

Population of rodents, cattles, bushes and shrubs in residential area and agricultural practices were the probable cause of the outbreak. Cases were treated symptomatically. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

status: under surveillance
comments: Cases were reported from Village Mandia Gaon, Sub-District Baghbor, District Barpeta. Cases presented with vomiting and loose watery stool. District RRT investigated the outbreak. The affected age group was 19-40 years. Three deaths were reported. A 40-year-old male died on 13.08.2025 with hypovolemic shock due to acute gastroenteritis, a 22-year-old male died on 14.08.2025 and a 37-year-old female died on 15.08.2025. 1 sample of uncooked pulse, two samples of uncooked rice, and water samples were collected. The uncooked masur dal and rice samples conformed to prescribed standards. However, the water sample was found unsafe— chemically (pH 6.2 against standard 6.5–8.5) and microbiologically (TNTC coliforms, E. coli detected). The kitchen area was observed to have inadequate hygiene, improper food storage, and lapses in cleanliness. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

status: under control
comments: Cases were reported from Tezpur urban area, Sub-District Tezpur, District Sonitpur. Cases presented with multiple episodes of vomiting and loose motion. District RRT investigated the outbreak. The affected age group was 2-76 years. Two families from the affected locality travelled to Bhopal for Sawan Pooja and returned on 12- 08-2025. Symptoms began the next day (13-08-2025) in all family members, indicating a possible exposure during travel or shortly after return. Cases were treated at Kanaklata Civil Hospital and Tezpur Medical College. All 21 stool samples collected and sent to TMCH for culture, showed growth of normal gut flora. Water samples collected were found unsatisfactory for drinking purpose. Alternate drinking water supply was arranged by PHED. Chlorination of water sources was done. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

comments: Cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from Village Malda (Malisahi hamlet), Sub-District Boriguma, District Koraput. District RRT investigated the outbreak. cases of fever, abdominal pain and vomiting were reported from village keifang venglai, keifang leitan, tawizo, tualbung & saitual i, sub- district phullen, district saitual. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house search for fever cases was done in the affected area. cases were treated at district hospital saitual. cases were in the age group 10 months - 60 years. out of 20 blood samples collected and sent to dh saitul, 20 samples tested positive for salmonella typhi by typhidot. chlorination of the water source was done. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation

cases were reported from bharathi excellence school, village mellahalli, chc k.m. doddi, sub-district maddur, district mandya. cases presented with symptoms of loose motion and vomiting. cases were in the age group 3 - 42 years. district and block rrt visited the school and conducted an inspection of the hostel and water supply. food prepared at the hostel was suspected to be contaminated. cases were treated at a health camp organized in the school premises. out of 3 water samples collected and sent to dphl mandya, 2 samples were non-potable. 3 stool samples were collected and tested at mims, mandya (hanging drop test) and 2 stool samples were collected and tested at mmc, mysore (culture and sensitivity) showed growth of no pathogens. iec activities were conducted. the education department was informed of preventive action in the school hostel. the school authorities, hostel warden, and headmaster were instructed to supply purified drinking water and ensure regular cleaning of the water tanks.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village marora, chc badagaon, sub-district/district jhansi. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house survey was done. people consumed water from 4 hand pumps in the locality. cases were given ors packets and zinc tablets.few cases were treated at mlb medical college jhansi. cases were in the age group 2-56 years. out of 4 water samples collected and sent to medical college jhansi, 2 samples were non-potable. all the four handpumps which were used in the village for drinking water were marked as unsafe for water consumption. chlorine tablets were distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village singraidih, sub-district tundi, district dhanbad. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated symptomatically. ors packets and zinc tablets were given to cases. chlorine tablets were distributed to the households. cases were in the age group 14-60 years. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.
under surveillance
comments: Cases of fever with rash were reported from Village Parudur, Sub-District Pattambi, District Palakkad. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases were students of a Madrassa. Symptomatic treatment was given to cases. Cases were in the age group of 6-10 years. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

status: cases were reported from village byalyal, phc huvina hipparagi, sub-district basavana bagewadi, district vijayapura. cases presented with symptoms of loose stool and vomiting. the most affected age group was 5-10 years. district rrt investigated the outbreak. there was a contaminated borewell near a stream, which was submerged due to rainfall and surrounded by open-air defecation sites considered as the possible source of water contamination. a temporary opd clinic was established at the anganwadi center for treatment of cases. out of 7 water samples collected and tested at dphl vijayapur, 3 samples were found unfit for human consumption. all 3 stool samples collected and tested at dphl vijayapur were negative for cholera with only normal commensals isolated. alternate water supply was arranged in the village. cleaning and chlorination of ohts and pipelines and bacteriological testing before water supply was done. drilling of a new borewell was done to ensure safe water. community awareness and health education was given regarding safe drinking water, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

cases were reported from galaxy inn brilliant girls hostel, village mutholy (ward no. 9), sub-district meenachil, district kottayam. cases presented with symptoms of fever, abdominal discomfort, nausea and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the cases were in the age group 18 to 23 years. cases were treated symptomatically. 2 water samples were collected from the hostel's well tank and filter unit and tested negative for e. coli and coliforms at kwa kottayam. improper food storage, inadequate reheating, or cross contamination during food handling may have contributed to the outbreak. ors packets were given to cases. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation.
under control
comments: Cases were reported from Village Borpa Block Magorra, CHC Goverdhan, District Mathura. Cases presented with loose motion, nausea and vomiting after eating golgappas. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases were treated symptomatically. 6 cases were treated at CHC Goverdhan. Most of the cases were children below 10 years of age. Community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe food habits, hygiene and sanitation

status: cases were reported from village ambehoi & dhamangaon, sub-district chalisgaon, district jalgaon. district rrt investigated the outbreak. active search for fever cases was done in the affected area. cases were treated at phc dahiwad. cases were in the age group 7-75 years. out of 47 blood samples collected and sent to gmc jalgaon, 4 samples tested positive for dengue (igm elisa). anti-larval activities and fogging was done in the affected villages. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.
under surveillance
comments: Cases were reported from Village Madappally, Sub-District Changanassery, District Kottayam. Cases presented with fever, cough and body pain. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases were students of Good Shepherd public school located in ward 17 of Madappally panchayath. Symptomatic treatment was given to cases. 4 cases were treated at FHC Madappally. Cases were in the age group 5-18 years. 3 nasopharyngeal swabs were sent to NIV ALP and all were negative for respiratory Viral panel. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on respiratory etiquette, hygiene and sanitation.

status: cases were reported from village narigram area, uphc akhlol jakatnaka, district bhavnagar. cases presented with symptoms of diarrhea, vomiting and severe dehydration.district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 5-44 years. cases were treated at a health camp organized in the locality. 1 stool sample collected and sent to sir t hospital tested negative for v. cholerae (culture). out of 3 water samples sent to government medical college bhavnagar, 2 samples were non-potable. chlorine tablets distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from the urban area of uphc/sub-district sidhpur, district patan. cases presented with symptoms of nausea and abdominal pain. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 5-25 years. cases were treated at uphc sidhpur. 2 water samples collected and sent to general hospital sidhpur were unsatisfactory for drinking purpose. all 16 blood samples collected and sent to sdh sidhpur tested positive for hepatitis a (igm elisa). chlorination of water sources was done in the locality. pipeline leakages were repaired by phed. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village devari baghelan, chc naigarhi district rewa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases presented with loose watery stool, abdominal pain, vomiting after eating "kondo rot" prasad at matarani temple. cases were treated at chc naigadhi. 1 patient was treated at medical college rewa. all cases recovered. 3 stool samples collected and sent to dphl rewa, tested negative for cholera by culture. cases were in the age group 3-70 years. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe food habits.

cases of fever with rash were reported from village pipradih, chc bermo, sub- district chandrapura, district bokaro. district rrt investigated the outbreak. active search for fever cases done in the locality. cases were in the age group 4 - 48 years. all cases were treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

cases were reported from village shankavarampalli, phc mittemari, sub- district bagepalli, district chikkaballapura. cases presented with symptoms of vomiting and loose motion. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 8 -78 years. nearly 400 people attended a death ceremony. the lunch served was contaminated with rainfall and the food preparation area was unhygienic. cases were treated symptomatically. 1 stool sample was collected and sent to dphl chikballapur showed growth of no pathogens. 2 water samples tested with h₂s media were found potable. community awareness and health education was given by peripheral team on safe food habits, hygiene and sanitation. house-to-house survey was done by health staff and asha workers to identify any new cases.
under surveillance
comments: Cases were reported from Village Kaimuria, Sub-District Pawai, District Panna. Cases presented with fever with chills. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Active search for fever cases was done. Cases were treated at CHC Pawai. Cases were in the age group 2-75 years. Out of 21 blood samples collected, 18 samples tested positive for P. falciparum by RDT. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

status: under control
comments: Cases were reported from Village Rengakathera, Block Ghumka, Sub-District Rajnandgaon, District Rajnandgaon. Cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. District RRT investigated the outbreak. Cases were treated symptomatically at a health camp established in the affected area. 5 cases were treated at GMCH Rajnandgaon. Chlorine tablets distributed to households. Community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

status: cases were reported from village kanagar, sub-district danta, district banas kantha. cases presented with symptoms of loose watery stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 5-60 years. cases were treated at chc danta. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to banas general hospital palanpur tested negative for cholera by culture. a 3-year- old male expired on 27.07.2025 with acute gastroenteritis, severe dehydration, shock and akd. chlorine tablets were distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases reported from village chaharon, sub center-munda khera, chc chhachhrauli, district yamuna nagar. the cases presented with symptoms of stomach pain, loose watery stools and headache. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated at a health camp organized in the locality and at chc chhachhrauli. all age groups ≥ 5 years were affected. 7 stool samples were collected and sent to dphl (idsp) yamuna nagar tested negative for cholera by culture. all 11 blood samples collected tested negative for hepatitis a & e (igm elisa). 4 water samples were collected and sent to dphl (idsp) for bacteriological test were found unfit for drinking purpose. ors packets were distributed. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village gharoa sonapur, bachachuba and khatikuchi, sub district sipajhar, district darrang. cases presented with abdominal pain, vomiting, loose motion, and fever. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the affected age group was 2-70 years. it was observed that the outbreak occurred due to consumption of unhygienic food served at a birthday party. all cases were treated. 8 rectal swabs and 1 water sample was collected and sent to iphl darrang. culture for rectal swab samples showed growth of normal gut flora. h₂s test on the water sample showed the water was potable. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose stool, abdominal pain, and vomiting were reported from village pudussery east, phc pudussery, sub- district palakkad, district palakkad. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were trainees in forest training institute, waalayar. cases were in the age group 24-40 years. 2 stool samples collected were negative for salmonella, shigella and vibrio cholera. cases treated symptomatically. health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene maintenance and proper sanitation.

cases of loose motion were reported from village chirmatekdi, phc bhaura, sub- district shahpur, district betul. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house survey done cases treated symptomatically by the peripheral team. 3 patients treated at chc shahpur. ors packets and zinc tablets given to cases. cases were in the age group 9-55 years. 5 water samples collected and sent to dphl bhopal were non-potable. chlorination of water sources was done in the village. health education on hand hygiene and safe drinking water given to the community by peripheral team.

cases of loose motion were reported from village chelasi, block nawalgarh, sub- district nawalgarh, district jhunjhunu. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the affected age group was 9-74 years. the cases consumed food at an engagement ceremony. cases treated symptomatically. out of 10 stool samples collected and sent to dphl jhunjhunu, 8 samples showed growth of staphylococcus aureus species. all 5 water samples collected were potable. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose motion, vomiting and abdominal pain were reported from village remanda, phc remunda, sub- district bheden, district bargarh. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were students of khiresara meher high school. cases were treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 11-15 years. ors packets were distributed to the cases. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe food habits and drinking water, hygiene maintenance and proper sanitation.

cases were reported from village patrapada, sub-district thakurmunda, district mayurbhanj. cases were students of patrapada ashram school who presented with loose motion, vomiting, nausea and abdominal pain after eating food prepared in the school kitchen. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. 4 cases treated at chc thakurmunda and 2 cases at sdh anadapur. cases were in the age group 7-19 years. three rectal swabs and three water samples were collected. water samples tested h₂s positive, with no residual chlorine detected on ot testing. rectal swabs showed no enteropathogenic organisms. all samples were tested at dphl, mayurbhanj.1 stool and 2 rectal swab samples collected and sent to dphl mayurbhanj tested negative for cholera by culture. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe food habits and hygiene maintenance.

cases of fever were reported from village ragauli, sub-district semaria, district rewa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house search of fever cases was done. cases were treated at chc semariya. all 3 blood samples collected and sent to virology lab rewa medical college tested positive for orientia tsutsugamushi (igm elisa). cases were in the age group 2-66 years. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, personal protection, hygiene and sanitation

cases of fever were reported from village ulahikala, sub-district gudh, district rewa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house search of fever cases was done. cases were treated at chc gudh. all 3 blood samples collected and sent to virology lab rewa medical college tested positive for orientia tsutsugamushi (igm elisa). cases were in the age group 3-67 years. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, personal protection, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose motion were reported from village nangalbod, sub-district sinapali, district nuapada. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house survey done in the village. symptomatic treatment given to cases. 3 cases treated at chc sinapali. cases were in the age group 5-60 years. all 3 stool samples collected and sent to dphl balangir tested negative for cholera by culture. all 5 water samples collected tested positive test. for h2s chlorine tablets were distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene maintenance and proper sanitation.

cases were reported from wakro township, sub- district wakro eac, district lohit. cases presented with fever and body aches. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the affected age group was 14-70 years. cases were treated at chc wakro and zonal general hospital tezu. 416 serum samples were collected and sent to dphl tezu where 61 samples were found positive for dengue, tested by igm elisa. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases reported from village mathiya, phc jiyanpura, sub-district sakaldiha, district chandauli. the cases presented with symptoms of loose motion and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the cases were of the age group of 1-55 years. out of 4 water samples collected and sent to regional lab swasthya bhawan lucknow, 1 sample was non-potable. people had consumed water from handpump in the affected area. chlorine tablets distributed to households. ors packets given to cases. health education given regarding safe drinking water and hygiene maintenance.

cases were reported from village changa, sub-district petlad, anand. the cases presented with loss of appetite, anorexia and abdominal pain. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 5-44 years. cases were treated at chc petlad. out of 11 blood samples tested at ssg hospital vadodara, 8 samples were positive for hepatitis a (igm elisa). out of 22 water samples collected and sent to s.s hospital petlad, 7 samples were unsatisfactory for drinking purpose. pipeline leakages were repaired by phed. chlorination of water sources was done in the locality. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village ugna sehrana, chc/sub-district karahal, district sheopur. district rrt investigated the outbreak. population of the affected area was 1034. house to house survey of 230 households was done. people consumed water from handpump and tubewell in the village. cases were treated at a health camp organized in the locality and at chc karahal. cases were in the age group 2 months - 65 years. 2 stool samples collected and sent to srvs medical college and hospital shivpuri tested negative for cholera (hanging drop preparation). 6 water samples collected by phed were satisfactory for drinking purpose. 1 death occurred in a 2-month- old female on 16.07.2025 with symptoms of fever and breathlessness, 1 death in a 10-month- old male on 18-07-2025 with symptoms of loose motion, vomiting and fever, 1 death in a 28-year-old-male on 20- 07-2025 with severe dehydration and shock and a 17 months old male died on 24-07-2025. chlorination of water sources was done by phed. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation

cases were reported from village badgaon mal, phc jawar, sub-district khandwa, district east nimar. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases presented with fever and joint pain. the most affected age group was 15-44 years. out of 12 serum samples collected and sent to district hospital khandwa, 11 samples tested positive for chikungunya (igm elisa). community awareness and health education were given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene, and sanitation. sources identified included water collection on concrete surfaces and plastic waste

cases were reported from n-able training institution, karimakkad ward-38, uphc thrikakara, district ernakulam. the cases presented with symptoms of loose stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the affected age group was 17 - 38 years. cases were treated symptomatically. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to niv alappuzha, which tested pcr negative for the viral panel. 2 water samples tested at rphl, ernakulam showed presence of coliforms. cleaning and chlorination of ohts was done. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mawriangtynnai , phc kynrud, block mairang, district eastern west khasi hills. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases presented with symptoms of vomiting, abdominal pain, headache, and loose stools. cases had consumed leftover food. cases were treated at phc kynrud and nongum. the 15–19 years age group was identified as the most affected. 1 death occurred in a 15-year-old female on 14.07.2025 with symptoms of multiple episodes of vomiting and loose stools. cause of death was age, shock with aspiration pneumonia. community awareness and health education on the importance of safe food and water handling practices was done by the peripheral team.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village garra (ct), sub- district lalbarra, district balaghat. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically at a health camp organized in the locality. ors packets and zinc tablets distributed to cases by asha and anm. 2 patients treated at a private hospital. cases were in the age group 2-77 years. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on use of boiled water for drinking purpose, hygiene maintenance and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mohan pada, phc mathalput, sub-district koraput, district koraput. cases are presented with fever, swelling and joint pain. cases were in the age group 5 - 73 years. district rrt and vbd team investigated the outbreak. aedes aegypti larvae were found in plastic drums and outdoor waste material in the community on investigation. all cases were treated at nearest health facilities. out of 16 serum samples collected and sent to rmrc, bhubaneswar, 10 samples tested positive for chikungunya (igm elisa). the population of the affected area was 1066. vector control measures undertaken in the community. iec-bcc activities on anti- chikungunya measures with focus on source reduction done to create awareness and to minimize the formation of breeding source followed by personal protection and cleaning of peri-domestic areas (cutting of wild bushes-shrubs) by asha, anm and health supervisor

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village durlavpur, sub- district saltora, district bankura. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated symptomatically. all 3 rectal swab samples collected and sent to bsmch bankura revealed common gut microbiota. all 5 drinking water samples collected from a well and tube well were contaminated with coliforms and e. coli. chlorine tablets were distributed to households. ors sachets and zinc tablets were given to cases. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village emlidhana, phc chirapatla, sub-district chicholi, district betul. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house survey was done. cases treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 1-60 years. 1 stool sample collected and sent to dphl bhopal was invalid. health education on hand hygiene and safe drinking water was given to the community by the peripheral team.

cases were reported from village kolari, sub-district iritty, district kannur..cases presented with symptoms of loss of appetite, abdominal pain and fever. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were in the age group 6 - 38 years. all the cases were diagnosed based on symptoms and abnormal liver test results. cases were treated at chc iritty. out of 6 water samples collected and sent to ral lab kuthuparamba, 3 samples were contaminated with coliform and e. coli. out of 2 blood samples collected and sent to indira gandhi co-operative hospital thalassery, 1 sample tested positive for hepatitis a (igm elisa). ors packets were given to cases. super chlorination of wells was done. community awareness and health education was given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village behl (111), sub-district balh, district mandi. cases presented with loose motions and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 4-85 years. out of 2 water samples collected and tested at slbsg mch nerchowk, 1 sample was non-potable. 2 stool samples collected showed growth of klebsiella species. chlorine tablets were distributed to households. community awareness and health education was conducted regarding hand washing, use of boiled water for drinking purpose, ors preparation, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose watery stool were reported from village phasiguda, phc bhismagiri, sub-district digapahandi, district ganjam. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. cases consumed stale food (kanji). two cases treated at chc digapahandi & 4 cases treated at mkcg medical college and hospital, berhampur odisha. one stool sample collected tested negative for cholera by culture. cases were in the age group 5-71 years. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene maintenance and proper sanitation.

cases were reported from village kuda (ct) & dhansiri, uphc duncan, district dimapur. cases presented with fever, headache altered mental status, unconscious and neck rigidity. district rrt investigated the outbreak. 4 csf samples of patients were confirmed for je (igm elisa) on 11th july (1 case) and 16th july (3 cases) respectively at sentinel surveillance site laboratory, district hospital dimapur. cases had no travel history. a 48-year-old female died on 12- 07-2025 before the test result of confirmed je was received. second death occurred in a 80 -year -old male on 30-07-2025 with bacterial meningitis. chronic hepatitis b and pulmonary tb were the other co- morbid conditions. door -to -door fever surveillance was conducted, and fogging operations were carried out in the locality. health education and community awareness were provided regarding je symptoms, prevention, and vector control measures.

cases of fever with rash were reported from village mudiya, para khas & para ugad, chc badagaon, sub-district badgaon dhasan, district tikamgarh.district rrt investigated the outbreak. active search for fever cases done in the locality. cases treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 2-8 years. all 4 serum samples collected and sent to icmr, jabalpur tested positive for measles (igm elisa). community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village katrihar, phc bagaia, sub-district chitrangi, district singrauli.district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. majority of the cases were in the age group 15-44 years. 1 stool sample collected and sent to dphl rewa tested negative for cholera by culture. 1 water sample was collected and sent to dphl rewa showed growth of coliform bacteria by culture. chlorine tablets distributed to households. ors packets and zinc tablets given to cases. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village badkud, chc/sub- district chitrangi, district singrauli. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 4-85 years. 1 water sample was collected and sent to dphl rewa showed growth of coliform bacteria by culture. chlorine tablets distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village amainagar, sub- district ghatshila. district east singhbhum. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated at a health camp organized in the village. cases were in the age group 2 - 65 years. ors packets were distributed to cases. 3 water samples were collected and sent to mgm vrdl lab jamshedpur showed presence of enterococcus faecalis. chlorine tablets were distributed to households. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation

cases were reported from village gidhauda, phc sarangpur, sub-district shahnagar, district panna. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases presented with loose motion. people consumed water from a borewell in the affected area. cases were treated at phc sarangpur. cases were in the age group 1-77 years. 1 water sample collected by phed was potable. chlorination of water sources done by phed. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene maintenance and sanitation.

cases were reported from village hardi, sub-district akaltara, district janjgir champa. cases presented with symptoms of loose motion and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. majority of the cases were in the age group 15-60 years. cases were treated symptomatically at a health camp organized in the locality. 1 stool sample was collected and sent to district hospital janjgir tested negative for cholera by culture. public health measures were taken, such as water chlorination by the phe department, distribution of chlorine tablets and bleaching powder, and health education on hand hygiene, consumption of boiled water. community awareness was done by the peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene maintenance and proper sanitation.

cases were reported from village mulavukad (ct), sub-district kanayannur, district ernakulam. the cases presented with symptoms of loose stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated at chc kanayannur. cases were in the age group 5 - 65 years. cases had consumed food from a bakery. 2 water samples were collected from the tap of bakery and sent to regional public health laboratory, ernakulam showed presence of coliforms. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on safe drinking water and food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village bungapali, sub-district ambabhona, district bargarh. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 2 rectal swabs and 3 water samples were collected and sent to dphl bargarh. rectal swabs did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village changera, sub-district rajpura, district patiala. cases presented with loose stools. 1 stool sample and 23 water samples were collected and sent dphl patiala and sphl. stool sample did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. 8 water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village chalakdih, sub district ghatshila, district east singhbum. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 8 water samples were collected and sent to mgm vrdl lab. water samples found unfit for potable purpose (e. coli and enterococcus faecalis detected). district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village ratai, sub-district ichagarh, district saraikela kharsawan. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village deodaha, sub-district dhanarua, district patna. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to pmch patna. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from sub- district pamgarh, district janjgir-champa. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to district hospital janjgir. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation. contaminated water might be the cause of outbreak.

cases were reported from villages bendri, dhaura bhata, sub-district bhatapara, district baloda bazar. cases presented with diarrhea and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation. contaminated water might be the cause of outbreak.

cases were reported from village karmadih, sub-district baloda bazar, district baloda bazar. cases presented with diarrhea and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation. contaminated water might be the cause of outbreak.

cases were reported from district gandhinagar. cases presented with loose stools. 4 water samples were collected and sent to gmers, gandhinagar. it was found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mandora, sub-district tral, district pulwama. cases presented with fever, fatigue and jaundice. 3 blood samples and 7 water samples were collected and sent to dphl pulwama. 2 blood samples found positive for hepatitis a by elisa, and 2 water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from talap town, kokaratoli gaon, sub- district doom dooma, district tinsukia. cases presented with fever, jaundice, loss of appetite. 33 blood samples were collected and sent to dphl tinsukiya. 7 samples found positive for the leptospirosis igm by elisa. however, all 33 samples found negative for hepatitis a and e. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village uttar kanchanpur, sub- district algapur, district hailakandi. cases presented with fever. 1 blood sample out of 24 samples found positive for malaria (plasmodium vivax) by pbf examination at kalingar bphc. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village shankarpur, sub- district isagarh, district ashoknagar. cases presented with fever with rash, vomiting. 6 blood samples were collected and sent for testing. all 6 blood samples found positive for measles igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. age group of the affected cases were 1-14 years. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mahipani, sub-district bisra, district sundargarh. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 2 rectal swabs and 6 water samples were collected and sent to dphl sundergarh. rectal swabs did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. all the cases developed the symptoms after having food in hostel meal. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village darshila, sub-district budar, district shahdol. cases presented with loose stools. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to gmc shahdol. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village kumhrauwa, sub- district kolaras, district shivpuri. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village kirikichi pali, sub- district manamunda, district boudh. cases presented with fever and jaundice. total 54 blood samples and 4 water samples were collected and sent to dphl boudh. 19 blood samples came positive for hepatitis a igm by elisa, and water samples found fit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village navaikulam, sub- district varkala, district thiruvananthapuram. cases presented with loose stools, pain abdomen and vomiting after taking meals in lunch at glps kizhakanela on 16-07- 2025. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated at gmch, klm. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village bamhori bansha, sub- district lalitpur, district lalitpur. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 3 water samples tested at regional lab swasthya bhawan lucknow and found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. affected age group was 3-60 years. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from villages thang meiband, uripok, lamphe lpat, singja mei & maibam leikai, sub-district lamphelpat, district imphal west. cases presented with fever, retro- orbital pain, body pain, myalgia and rash. district rrt and block team visited the affected area. fever surveillance and source reduction activities were carried out in the affected localities. cases were treated at health camps organized in the villages. cases were in the age group 15-44 years. 396 serum samples collected and sent to vrdl- rims and vrdl-jnims tested positive for dengue (ns1, igm elisa). 840 samples tested positive for dengue by rapid ns1 ag and 15 samples tested positive by rapid igm. fogging in the affected areas done every week, larvicide spray on alternate days, aedes larvae survey by malaria health workers was carried out, itbn, emptying and cleaning water containers, flower pots, vase done weekly. health education and iec activities done by the health workers.

cases were reported from village begarwani khurd, sub- district keolari, district seoni. cases presented with loose stools. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from nangeli ayurveda college, nelikuzhy, village eramalloor (ct), sub-district kothamangalam, district ernakulam. cases presented with fever, loose stools and vomiting. 2 water samples were collected and sent to rph lab ernakulam. both water samples found unfit for potable purpose (coliforms detected). district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from poonga nagar, brindhavan nagar, uphc vilankurichi, sub-district coimbatore south, district coimbatore. cases presented with fever with joint pain. total 5 blood samples found negative for chikungunya igm by elisa at dphl pollachi. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from kps school, aggal hsc, kattabettu phc, sub-district kotagiri, district the nilgiris. cases presented with fever with rash. 2 blood samples were collected and sent to dphl nilgiris. sample were negative for dengue, leptospirosis, typhoid and malaria. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from shalimar garden area, chc tarapuridistrict meerut. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. district rrt investigated the outbreak. all the cases developed the symptoms after having food in a marriage function. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation

cases were reported from village chotaudali, sub- district barhait, district sahebganj. during fever survey, 9 blood samples found positive for malaria by rdt kits. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated with anti-malarial. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village gomta, sub-district wadhwan, district surendranagar. cases presented with jaundice. total 2 blood samples and 2 water samples were collected and sent to mgs general hospital, surendranagar. both blood samples came positive for hepatitis a igm by elisa, and water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

case was reported from village kumaramputhur, sub-district mannarkad, district palakkad. a 57-year-old male presented with fever, vomiting. blood sample found positive for nipah by rt-pcr at vrdl manjeri and niv, pune. district rrt investigated the outbreak. case was admitted in the hospital. he expired on 17-07- 2025. due to illness. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from health facility maranchery, sub-district ponnani, district malappuram. cases presented with loose stools, pain abdomen and vomiting after attending a marriage function. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mundakayam, sub- district kanjirappally, district kottayam. cases presented with symptoms of jaundice. 7 blood samples tested positive for hepatitis a by elisa from gh kaniirapally. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village rosekandi grant, bphc dholai, sub-district silchar, district cachar. cases presented with fever. 5 blood samples found negative for malaria by pbf examination at borjalanga mphc lab. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village gayatri nagar, sub- district visakhapatnam (urban), district visakhapatanam. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 2 stool samples and 5 water samples were collected and sent to regional laboratory. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. 3 water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from sub- district gangajalghati, district bankura. cases presented with loose stools. 4 rectal swabs and 1 water sample were collected and sent to bsmch, bankura. all the rectal swabs found positive for vibrio cholerae on culture and water sample found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated in the hospital. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village bamhauri, sub-district sirmour, district rewa. cases presented with loose stools. 5 stool samples were collected and sent to dphl rewa. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation. a 95- year-old female expired on 16/07/2025.

cases of fever with rash, cough and cold were reported from village addada & bomlapura, sub-district koppa, district chikkamagaluru. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were students of prabhodhini gurukula school. cases were treated at th koppa. cases were in the age group 6-18 years. 5 serum samples collected and sent to hims hassan, tested negative for measles rubella (igm elisa). 13 serum samples collected and sent to dphl, chikkamagalur tested negative for dengue (ns1 elisa). source reduction activities were done. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases of fever with rash were reported from village zedua, sub-district longding hq, district longding. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases presented with symptoms of fever with rash. active search for fever cases was done in the affected area. cases were treated symptomatically. out of 15 blood samples collected and sent to gmc guwahati assam, 5 samples tested positive for measles (igm elisa). vitamin a supplementation was provided. cases were in the age group 6-15 years. all the cases were unvaccinated for measles.. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, vaccine- preventable diseases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

cases of loose motion and vomiting were reported from village butwa, phc khatai, sub-district karahal, district singrauli. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house to house survey was done. cases were treated symptomatically. cases were in the age group 2-70 years.community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village makrohar, sub-district singrauli, district singrauli. cases presented with loose stools. 1 stool sample and 1 water sample were collected and sent to dphl rewa. stool sample did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. water sample found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from kenwade, phc pimpalgaon, tal kagal, district kolhapur cases presented with fever, loss of appetite, jaundice. 40 blood samples and 10 water samples were collected and sent to rh solankpur. eight samples tested positive for hepatitis a igm by elisa. three water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from sub- district sainkul, district kendujhar. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 2 rectal swabs and 6 water samples were collected and sent to dphl kendujhar. rectal swabs did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. all the cases developed the symptoms after consuming local mushroom in the dinner. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village atapur, sub district hasanpur. district samastipur. cases presented with fever with rash. 5 blood samples were collected and sent to aiims patna. all the samples found negative for the measles igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. age group of the affected cases were 4-30 years. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vaccination, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village rapkata, sub-district balarampur, district purulia. cases presented with loose stools. 2 rectal swabs and 1 water sample were collected and sent to bsmch, bankura. rectal swabs found positive for vibrio cholerae on culture and water sample found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated in the hospital. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village badgaon mafi, sub- district burhanpur, district burhanpur. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 5 rectal swabs and 2 water samples were collected and sent to dphl indore. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism, water sample found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village mayyanad (ct), sub- district kollam, district kollam. cases presented with symptoms of jaundice, fever, headache and fatigue. 7 blood samples tested positive for hepatitis a by elisa, 5 water samples tested and found unfit for potable purpose (e. coli detected). district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village kuthur, sub-district nawagarh, district janjgir- champa. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. 2 stool samples were collected and sent to district hospital janjgir. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation. contaminated water might be the cause of outbreak.

cases were reported from sub- district jhabua, district jhabua. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 2 water samples were collected and sent to dphl indore; samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation. two children, 5 year old male and 8 year old male expired on 11/07/2025 due to severe dehydration.

cases were reported from villages karehalli, thirupathi & bommanahalli, sub-district arsikere, district hassan. cases presented with loose stools, vomiting and abdominal discomfort. 5 stool samples and 2 water samples were collected and sent to dphl hassan. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism, water sample found unfit for potable purpose district rrt investigated the outbreak. all the cases developed the symptoms after attending a social gathering on 13th july, 2025. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village panapuzha, sub-district payyannur, district kannur. cases presented with symptoms of jaundice. 7 blood samples tested positive for hepatitis a by elisa, 9 water samples tested and found fit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases of loose watery stool were reported from village rapkata, block balarampur, district purulia. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated at bphc bansgarh. cases were in the age group 4-75 years. 2 rectal swab samples collected and sent to bsmch, bankura showed growth of vibrio cholerae by culture. 1 water sample collected by phed from tubewell was contaminated. water tanker was arranged by the bdo office. disinfection of tubewell was done. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on safe drinking water, hygiene and sanitation

cases of fever less than 7 days, headache and myalgia were reported from village vadasserikkara, kap-5 police battalion ward 8, chc ranny perunad, district pathanamthitta. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated at chc ranny perunad and ranni taluk hospital. 2 throat swab samples collected and sent to niv alappuzha, tested positive for influenza a (h3n2). cases were in the age group 24-51 years. health education given to the trainees in the camp by the district surveillance officer on spread of influenza and preventive measures, hygiene and sanitation.
under surveillance
comments: Cases of fever with myalgia were reported from Village Lagam, Sub- District Mulchera, District Gadchiroli. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house fever survey was done in the affected area. Cases were treated at PHC Lagam TR. Out of 190 blood samples collected and sent to Sentinel lab Gadchiroli, 16 samples tested positive for Dengue (NS1 ELISA). Cases were in the age group 4-65 years. 2 deaths occurred in a 45 year old female on 11-08-2025 at GMC Chandrapur and a 55 year old male died on 13-08-2025 at a private hospital. Vector control measures were undertaken. Community awareness was done on wearing full slevee clothes and use of mosquito nets.

status: cases were reported from village vekari, sub-district danta, district banas kantha. cases presented with loose stools. 1 stool sample was collected and sent to banas medical college and hospital. it came positive for cholera. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation. a 15- year-old male expired on 14- 07-2025.

cases were reported from village bapsai, tq kalyan, district thane. cases presented with loose stools. 6 water samples were collected and sent to bhujal sarvekshan vikas yantrana and found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village karhi tola, sub- district budar, district shahdol. cases presented with loose stools. 4 stool samples were collected and sent to gmc shahdol. stool samples did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village gobindpur & chota govindpur, sub-district golmuri-cum-jugsalai, district east singhbum. cases presented with fever with body ache. 18 blood samples found positive for the dengue ns1, by elisa at mgm college and hospital. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on vaccination, hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from chhatra bazar behera sahi ward no-38, sub-district cuttack, district cuttack. cases presented with vomiting and loose stools. 2 rectal swabs and 6 water samples were collected and sent to dphl cuttack. rectal swabs did not show growth of any pathogenic organism. water samples found unfit for potable purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases of fever with rash were reported from village deoli, sub-district chalisgaon, district jalgaon. cases were students of ashram shala deoli tal. district rrt investigated the outbreak. cases were treated symptomatically.all 6 serum samples collected tested positive for measles (igm elisa). cases were in the age group 6-13 years. community awareness and health education given by the peripheral team on isolation of cases, hygiene maintenance and proper nutrition.

a case was reported from engandiyur panchayath, sub-district chavakkad, district thrissur. district rrt investigated the outbreak. a 33 -year -old male presented with fever, sore throat, cough on 03/07/2025, followed by vesicles on hands and fingers the next day. the case had a history of travel from abroad on 14/07/2025. the patient was admitted to aster medicity, kochi on 15-07-2025, with clinical symptoms provisionally diagnosed as mpox. the case was diagnosed at aster medicity, kochi, and the sample was tested at niv alappuzha on 16-07-2025 which was positive for mpox by pcr. for confirmation, sample was further sent to niv pune on 18-07-2025, which was positive for mpox (clade i). the patient was cured and discharged from isolation at government medical college, kalamassery, ernakulam on 02.08.2025. community awareness and health education on control, hygiene, and sanitation.

cases were reported from village badalbheta, sub-district doom dooma, district tinsukia, under kakopather bphc. cases presented with loose stools with blood. the outbreak was linked to the consumption of food during a religious ceremony on 02/07/2025. all age groups were affected. three water samples and two stool samples were collected and sent to dphl tinsukia for culture and sensitivity testing. stool samples showed growth of shigella (non- lactose fermenting gram-negative bacilli), while mpn results confirmed that the water samples were unsatisfactory for drinking. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house-to- house active case search was conducted and treatment was given. mmu was deployed for case management. iec activities such as miking and health talks were conducted on sanitation, hygiene, and safe drinking water practices.

cases were reported from village ghivanda, sub-district jawhar, district palghar. cases presented with symptoms of fever. a total of 791 smear samples were collected and sent to mrhru dahanu, where 11 samples tested positive for plasmodium vivax. district rrt investigated the outbreak. rapid fever survey, mass slide collection, and entomological surveillance were conducted. temporary breeding sites were identified and destroyed, guppy fish were introduced, and fogging was carried out in the affected areas. health education sessions were organized to promote dry-day observance and vector- borne disease prevention. mass surveillance was carried out.

cases were reported from village chimnipada, sc shegave under phc palsun, sub-district nawapur, district nandurbar. cases presented with symptoms of loose motions. stool samples of two patients were sent for laboratory testing and were found negative for vibrio cholerae. water samples were sent to dphl. district rrt investigated the outbreak. daily active surveillance was done. community awareness and health education given by peripheral team on control measures, hygiene and sanitation. chlorination was done in the village, an alternative water supply was arranged.

cases were reported from village sagnapur, sub-district chamorshi, district gadchiroli. cases presented with symptoms of loose motion and vomiting on 10/07/2025. a total of 2 stool samples were collected and sent to hindlab gadchiroli, all tested negative for any entero-pathogen. district rrt investigated the outbreak. surveillance was started and iec activities on add were conducted. the cause of the outbreak was attributed to a pipeline leakage.

cases were reported from village dhirol, sub-district potka, district east singhbhum. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. two water samples were collected and sent to vrdl, out of which one sample was found unfit for potable purposes with enterococcus faecalis detected. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the cases were treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education were provided by the peripheral team on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village purwa, sub-district sirmour, district rewa. cases presented with symptoms of loose watery stool. the affected age group was 5 to 44 years. a total of 6 stool samples and 10 water samples were collected and sent to dphl dh rewa; stool samples showed no growth, but water samples were found contaminated on culture. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house-to-house surveys were conducted, treatment was provided, and a health camp was organized in the village for treatment. health education was given, and chlorination and bleaching were carried out in drinking water sources. the cause of the outbreak was contaminated drinking water. two deaths (45/f and 30/f) have occurred due to the illness on 12.08.2025.

cases were reported from village rajgarh, bhundaha and panni, sub- district sirmour, district rewa. cases presented with symptoms of fever and headache. the affected age group was 15 to 44 years. total 21 blood samples were collected and sent to virology lab, ssmc rewa, all samples were positive for scrub typhus igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house-to-house surveillance was done. community awareness and health education were provided by the peripheral team on control measures, hygiene, and sanitation.

a case was reported from sub-district dimapur sadar, district dimapur. a 36- year-old male presented with headache, fever, seizure, unconsciousness, neck rigidity, altered sensorium, restlessness and tongue bite. blood sample tested positive for je igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. distribution of necessary medications, continuous surveillance and community awareness activities on vector control measures, hygiene and sanitation were done. the patient died due to the illness on 15-07-2025 at district hospital dimapur.

cases were reported from village dar mohall herpora, jaggerpora block, district kupwara. cases presented with rash and fever. the affected age group was 2 to 44 years. a total of four blood samples were collected and sent to dphl kupwara for testing, all of which were found positive for chickenpox igm elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. health education was imparted regarding the spread of the disease and iec activities were conducted through miking.

cases were reported from village mohanpur, sub-district gobindpur, district dhanbad. cases presented with loose stools and vomiting. six water samples were collected and sent for testing. district rrt investigated the outbreak. the cases were treated symptomatically. community awareness and health education were carried out by the peripheral team focusing on hygiene and sanitation.

cases were reported from village manacadu, sub-district thiruvananthapuram, district thiruvananthapuram. cases presented with diarrhea and vomiting after consumption of food from a common place on 11-07-2025. one stool sample was collected and showed no growth of any entero-pathogen on culture at sphl thiruvananthapuram. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house-to- house surveillance and active case search were carried out, and community awareness and health education were given by the peripheral team on food safety, hygiene, and sanitation.

a case of je was reported from pachim bhadragaon, bidyapur under bongaigaon bphc, district bongaigaon. a 59-year- old female, presented with fever, seizures, and altered mental status. a csf sample was collected and tested at bongaigaon dphl, which was positive for je igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. house-to- house surveys were conducted to identify additional cases with similar symptoms. intensive community awareness activities were carried out regarding vector-borne disease prevention and control measures. the patient was unvaccinated against je and died on 17th july 2025.

cases were reported from polagam, village t.r. pattinam & karaikal, sub- district karaikal, district karaikal. cases presented with fever. the affected age group was 19-48 years. eight blood samples were collected and sent to dphl, gh karaikal, out of which seven were positive for dengue igm by elisa. the likely cause of the outbreak was water storage in uncovered barrels, leading to mosquito breeding. district rrt investigated the outbreak. source reduction activities, fogging, iec material distribution, and health awareness talks in the affected areas was done.

cases were reported from batgharia shc under dhemaji bphc, district dhemaji. cases presented with symptoms of fever, joint pain, body swelling, weakness, and headache, affecting individuals aged 6 to 55 years. six blood samples were collected and sent to rmrc-icmr ne region, dibrugarh, out of which 2 were positive for chikungunya igm by elisa. district rrt investigated the outbreak. community awareness, health education on vector control measures, hygiene, and sanitation.

cases were reported from village baitari, sub-district saraipali, district mahasamund. cases presented with diarrhea and vomiting. the most affected age group was 15-44 years. seven stool samples were collected and sent to hamar lab, pandri raipur, department of microbiology, for culture, all of which were found negative for any entero- pathogen. three water samples were also sent for testing, with coliform detected in one sample. a health camp and door-to- door survey were organized. district rrt investigated the outbreak. community awareness and health education were provided by the peripheral team on control measures, hygiene, and sanitation. contaminated water was suspected to be the cause of outbreak.

cases were reported from village chintoli, sub-district syalde, district almora. cases in the age group of 5-15 years presented with fever, vomiting, abdominal pain, weakness and jaundice. three blood samples were collected & sent to chc deghat, almora, which tested negative for hepatitis a. district rrt investigated the outbreak. health camp, door-to-door visits, counseling, prevention, and control activities were undertaken. active case search was conducted through house-to-house surveillance and iec activities were carried out on hygiene and sanitation.

a case was reported from village chidimit namesa, phc dagal, sub-district songsak, district east garo hills. a 31-year-old male presented with symptoms of fever, headache, muscle spasms, aerophobia, hyper salivation, insomnia, and hydrophobia. the case was clinically diagnosed as rabies. based on history, the exposure occurred approximately five months prior, but the patient did not seek care or initiate treatment from any health facility. the patient was admitted to williamnagar civil hospital on 7th july 2025 and succumbed to the illness on 10th july 2025. district rrt investigated the outbreak. as a precautionary measure, family members who were involved in patient care were advised to undergo post- exposure prophylaxis (pep). additionally, iec activities were conducted, including a public health awareness campaign in the presence of the local headman, in collaboration with the district veterinary department, which organized a mass awareness programme in chidimit namesa village.

cases were reported from ward no. 42, nanak nagar area, district jammu. cases presented with fever, stomach ache, vomiting and jaundice. a total of 5 blood samples were sent to gandhi nagar hospital, all 5 were positive for hepatitis a igm by elisa. three water samples were collected and sent for microbiological investigations to dphl gandhinagar, the results of which were unsatisfactory for drinking purpose. district rrt investigated the outbreak and community awareness and health education were conducted by the peripheral team on hygiene, sanitation, control measures, and safe drinking and eating habits.

cases were reported from village pratap ghar t.e. 126-225 under biswanath bphc, sub-district biswanath, district biswanath. cases presented with symptoms of fever, body ache, and rashes on the body. the most affected age group ranged from 19 to 54 years. all cases were diagnosed clinically. district rrt investigated the outbreak, conducted health education, and promoted hygiene and control measures. awareness campaigns and active case finding were carried out, with continuous follow-up. supportive medications were provided, and the affected area was marked as isolated.
